title: Hieracium cirritiforme
taxonomy: Hieracium cirritiforme is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a type of hawkweed, which is a large and diverse group of plants. The plant belongs to the genus Hieracium and is further classified within the tribe Cichorieae. Hieracium cirritiforme is native to North America and is found in alpine and subalpine regions. The exact classification of Hieracium cirritiforme may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research.

intro: Hieracium cirritiforme, commonly known as Hawkweed or Hieracium, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium cirritiforme include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Hieracium cirritiforme is prepared as a tea, tincture, or inflorescence-based preparation, which is used to treat various health conditions, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
